economic_notes: Subsequent to year end, the First Nation became a shareholder of Cedar Leaf Capital, Canada's first majority Indigenous-owned investment dealer, a venture between Scotiabank, Chippewas of Rama First Nation and two other Indigenous groups. Williams Treaty Settlement annual payout of $1,358,356 included in surplus. Large portfolio investments held by Scotiatrust (~$191M). Casino Rama ground rent and fees are significant revenue sources.
notes: Consolidated entity includes 10 fully consolidated subsidiaries and 3 GBEs (Rama Parking Inc., Casino Rama Inc., My Lucky Spot - Entertainment Centre Ltd. at 50%) accounted for under modified equity method. Large portfolio investments (~$191M) held by Scotiatrust. Restated prior year figures due to adoption of PS 3450 Financial Instruments on April 1, 2022. Williams Treaty Settlement distributions of $1,358,356 included in annual surplus. ISC funding calculated as sum of INAC contributions (842,613+233,296+4,394,289+2,561,003=8,031,201). expenses_infrastructure is sum of Operations and Maintenance ($9,555,247) plus Water and Waste Water ($1,991,103). Remuneration schedule not included in extracted text. Segment data partially cut off in source document.
